requests.replyPlaceholder: Write a reply to the student...

curriculumMap.title: Curriculum map — learning outcomes
curriculumMap.subtitle: Create and edit course learning outcomes (CLOs) per subject.
curriculumMap.noSubjectsForFilters: No subjects match the selected filters. Adjust filters or add subjects.
curriculumMap.aboutBody: Define CLOs here so instructors see them when mapping lessons and assessments. Instructors can view coverage in their curriculum map but cannot edit CLO definitions.

rubricBuilder.crit1L4: Ideas are clear and highly developed, with strong supporting examples and deep analysis.
rubricBuilder.crit1L3: Ideas are mostly clear with adequate examples and some analysis.
rubricBuilder.crit1L2: Basic ideas with limited examples and surface-level analysis.
rubricBuilder.crit1L1: Ideas unclear or missing, without examples or analysis.
rubricBuilder.crit2L4: Excellent logical structure with clear introduction and conclusion and smooth transitions.
rubricBuilder.crit2L3: Clear structure with some good transitions.
rubricBuilder.crit3L4: Professional academic language with varied vocabulary and very few grammar errors.
rubricBuilder.crit3L3: Good language with minor errors that do not affect meaning.
rubricBuilder.crit3L2: Repeated errors that sometimes affect clarity.
rubricBuilder.crit4L4: Full correct APA citation with a comprehensive reference list.
rubricBuilder.crit4L3: Good documentation with minor formatting errors.
rubricBuilder.instructorHint: Instructors attach this rubric to assessments from Create assessments — they do not edit the matrix here.
rubricBuilder.confirmDelete: Delete this rubric? This cannot be undone.
rubricBuilder.errCodeRequired: Rubric code is required (unique identifier, e.g. academic_writing_default).

academicYears.title: Academic Years
academicYears.subtitle: Manage academic years and control system-wide settings
academicYears.createSubtitle: Add a new academic year to the system
academicYears.currentYearExplainer: The official current operating year is not chosen on this screen. After you create the year, move it to Scheduled, open its detail page, then use Set as Current — that marks it as the active year (Is Current) and sets status to In Progress.
academicYears.universityWideDesc: If checked, this academic year will be available across all colleges
academicYears.name: Name
academicYears.nameHint: The display name for this academic year
academicYears.codeHint: Unique identifier for this academic year
academicYears.descriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of this academic year
academicYears.initialStatusHint: Use Draft while planning, or Scheduled when ready for the next step. "Current" is set later from the year’s detail page (Set as Current), not here.
academicYears.initialSystemControlFlagsDesc: These flags act as master switches and can be changed later via lifecycle actions
academicYears.autoGenerateDesc: When you select start and end dates, the system can automatically generate the Name and Code based on the date range (e.g., "Academic Year 2024-2025" and "2024-2025").
academicYears.autoGenerateError: Please select both start and end dates first.
academicYears.operationsDisabledHint: Registration, grades, attendance, and financial posting are all turned off. This is not about missing year fields—open the year and turn on the switches when you are ready.
academicYears.lifecycleHelp: Lifecycle order: Draft → Scheduled → (when ready) Set as Current, which marks this year as the active one and sets status to In Progress. System control flags below are separate operational switches.
academicYears.isCurrentHelp: Shows whether this is the designated current operating year. Use Set as Current above when the year is Scheduled or In Progress.
academicYears.setCurrentConfirm: Set this academic year as current?
academicYears.cloneConfirm: Create a draft copy of this academic year?
academicYears.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this academic year? This action cannot be undone.

requirementsHierarchy.majorBody: Catalog totals here should match your degree plan. When you save the degree plan, total credits and minimum graduation GPA are synced to this major for reports and listings.
requirementsHierarchy.degreePlanBody: This degree plan (major sheet) is the official source for students: total credits, minimum GPA for graduation, and course groups. Saving updates the major’s catalog totals to stay in sync.

degreePlanSheet.title: Configure Degree Plan - {{majorName}}
degreePlanSheet.subtitle: Define the academic structure and course requirements for this major
degreePlanSheet.loadSheetDetailsFailed: Failed to load degree plan details
degreePlanSheet.noSubjectsTitle: No Subjects Found for This Major
degreePlanSheet.noSubjectsDesc: You need to create subjects for this major first before configuring the degree plan.
degreePlanSheet.sheetTypeRuleBasedHint: Students can choose courses based on rules (e.g., choose 3 from 10)
degreePlanSheet.sheetTypeFixedHint: All courses are predefined by year/semester, no student choice
degreePlanSheet.minCreditsPerSemesterHint: Minimum credit hours required per semester for this major
degreePlanSheet.maxCreditsPerSemesterHint: Maximum credit hours allowed per semester for this major
degreePlanSheet.creditHoursNote: Note: This college uses Semester Settings as the source for credit hour rules. Credit hour limits are configured per semester (Create/Edit Semester), not in the major sheet. These fields are shown for reference but will not be used for validation.
degreePlanSheet.courseGroupsConfigDesc: Configure the 5 course groups with their rules and courses. Each group defines what students must complete.

colleges.universityWideAutoVisible: University-wide academic years, semesters, departments, and majors are automatically visible to all colleges—no import needed. New colleges will see them immediately.

generalSettings.useUniversitySettingsDesc: If checked, this college will use the university-wide settings instead of custom settings
generalSettings.noCollegeTypes: No college types configured in university settings. Using default types.
generalSettings.accreditationInfoPlaceholder: Accreditation details, accrediting bodies, dates, etc.
generalSettings.createLoginAccount: Create login account for college admin
generalSettings.createLoginAccountDesc: This will automatically create a login account so the college admin can access their portal at /login/college
generalSettings.adminNameHint: Leave empty to use Dean Name or College Name + "Admin"
generalSettings.adminEmailHint: Will use Contact Email or Official Email if left empty
generalSettings.adminPasswordHint: The admin will use this password to log in
generalSettings.idFormatPlaceholderHint: Tokens: {prefix}, {year}, {year2}, {college_code}, {sequence:D4} (change D4 to D5–D6 for width). College code is this college’s short code above.
generalSettings.localizationNoteTitle: Where to set language, time zone, and currency
generalSettings.localizationNoteSystem: Default language, time zone, and RTL options are configured in the System tab (Localization).
generalSettings.localizationNoteFinance: Currency code, symbol, and decimal places are configured only in the Financial tab — not here — so finance stays the single source of truth.

academicSettings.fromUniversitySettings: From University Settings (Common for All Colleges)
academicSettings.gradingScaleAndCreditsNote: Grading scale and semester credit limits are configured in University Settings and apply to all colleges. Configure these in University Settings → Academic.
academicSettings.gradingScaleDesc: Letter grades and grade points. These apply to all colleges.

financialSettings.tapPaymentApiKeyPlaceholder: Enter your TAP Payment API key
financialSettings.tapPaymentSecretKeyPlaceholder: Enter your TAP Payment secret key
financialSettings.testModeDesc: Enable test mode for payment gateway
financialSettings.financialMilestonesDesc: Financial milestones are calculated per semester based on payment percentage. Rules are dynamically applied: PM10 (10%) allows login and course access, PM30 (30%) allows attendance, PM60 (60%) allows exams, PM100 (100%) allows full access including grades.
financialSettings.milestonesCalculatedPerSemester: Note: Milestones are calculated per semester. Each semester has its own payment status.
financialSettings.currencySingleSourceNote: This is the only place to set currency for this college. Fees and invoices use these values.
financialSettings.financialMilestonesDesc: Financial milestones are calculated per semester based on payment percentage. Rules are dynamically applied: PM10 (10%) allows login and course access, PM30 (30%) allows attendance, PM60 (60%) allows exams, PM100 (100%) allows full access including grades.
financialSettings.milestonesCalculatedPerSemester: Note: Milestones are calculated per semester. Each semester has its own payment status.

emailSettings.smtpPortTypo586: 586 is not a standard SMTP port (often a typo for 587). Gmail uses 587 with STARTTLS, or 465 with implicit TLS. Wrong ports cause connection timeouts.
emailSettings.fromEmailAddressHint: Email address that will appear as sender
emailSettings.enableEmailNotificationsDesc: Send email notifications for system events
emailSettings.testEmailRequired: Enter a recipient address for the test email.
emailSettings.testEmailSent: Test email sent. Check the inbox (and spam folder).
emailSettings.testEmailFailed: Could not send the test email.
emailSettings.supabaseAuthSmtpHintTitle: Applicant OTP, magic links, and password reset emails
emailSettings.supabaseAuthSmtpHintBody: Those messages are sent by Supabase Auth using your hosted project’s SMTP settings, not automatically from the values saved here. To send them from your university address (instead of noreply@mail.app.supabase.io), open the Supabase Dashboard for this project → Authentication → SMTP settings → enable custom SMTP, and enter the same host, port, credentials, and sender name or email as below.
emailSettings.supabaseAuthSmtpHintLink: Supabase guide: Auth with custom SMTP
emailSettings.supabaseOtpEmailHintTitle: Show the 6-digit code in auth emails
emailSettings.supabaseOtpEmailHintBody: The default “Magic link” template often only shows a button or URL, so users never see an OTP. In the Supabase Dashboard → Authentication → Email templates → Magic link, include the one-time password (the docs call this the Token variable) in the body so applicants can type the code on any device. This repo’s supabase/templates/magic_link.html is a ready-made example for local dev; paste the same content into the hosted project.

systemSettings.sessionTimeoutMinutesHint: User session will expire after this period of inactivity
systemSettings.maxFileUploadSizeHint: Maximum size for file uploads in megabytes
systemSettings.timeZoneHint: Used for schedules and timestamps (e.g. UTC, Asia/Kuwait).

createInvoicePage.title: Create Invoice
createInvoicePage.subtitle: Create a new invoice for a student
createInvoicePage.feeHierarchyTitle: How fees connect (fee types, fee structures, major)
createInvoicePage.feeHierarchyIntro: Use this page together with Finance Configuration and each major’s catalog amounts.
createInvoicePage.feeHierarchyBullet1: Fee types define categories and rules (e.g. whether a semester is required when creating an invoice).
createInvoicePage.feeHierarchyBullet2: Fee structures (Finance → Configure base fees) hold priced rows for a college, optionally scoped by semester, major, and degree level.
createInvoicePage.feeHierarchyBullet3: Major fields (registration, tuition, lab) are program defaults—especially for application/registration payments.
createInvoicePage.feeHierarchyBullet4: Public registration charges the major registration fee first, then falls back to admission/registration fee structures. Manual invoices here should match those sources when possible.
createInvoicePage.majorCatalogNone: This student’s major has no registration fee set in the catalog. Configure it on the major or use a fee structure below.
createInvoicePage.lineFromMajorRegistrationDesc: Amount taken from the major’s registration fee field.
createInvoicePage.selectCollegeContinue: Please select a college to continue
createInvoicePage.searchStudentPlaceholder: Search by student ID, name, phone, or email…
createInvoicePage.feeStructuresHint: Select one or more fee structures to auto-fill line items. You can edit names and amounts afterward.
createInvoicePage.feeStructuresSemesterNote: Select a semester to see semester-based fee structures; non-semester fees appear without a semester.
createInvoicePage.feeStructuresSelectedOne: 1 fee structure selected. Editable items are highlighted in blue.

createInvoicePage.noFeeStructuresPrompt: Choose an invoice type and semester (if required), or add line items manually below.
createInvoicePage.semesterHelp: Fees are tied to the semester. Milestones (30%, 60%, etc.) are calculated per semester.
createInvoicePage.paymentAdmin: Admin payment (mark as paid by admin)
createInvoicePage.adminPaymentNote: Records you as verifier and marks the invoice paid. The student can access the portal immediately.

instructorPortal.commTplAssignmentReminderBody:
Dear students,

This is a reminder that {{assignmentName}} for {{courseCode}} ({{courseName}}) is due on {{dueDate}}.

Please submit your work on time through the student portal. Contact me if you have questions.

Best regards,
{{instructorName}}
instructorPortal.commTplGradePublicationBody:
Dear students,

Grades for {{courseCode}} ({{courseName}}) have been published in the student portal.

Please review your results. If you believe there is an error, contact me within the deadline stated in the course policy.

{{instructorName}}
instructorPortal.commTplResultsAnnouncementBody:
Dear students,

The results for {{courseCode}} ({{courseName}}) are now available.

Log in to the student portal to view your grades and feedback. Congratulations to everyone who passed, and best wishes for continued success.

{{instructorName}}

instructorPortal.cloAlignmentGap: {{codes}} — missing lecture or assessment alignment. Link CLOs when building lessons and creating assessments.
instructorPortal.assessmentCloLinkHint: Select which course learning outcomes this assessment measures.
instructorPortal.lessonCloLinkHint: Tick the CLOs this lecture covers, then click Save lesson content.
instructorPortal.curriculumReferencesPdfOnly: Please upload a PDF file only.
instructorPortal.analyticsCloEmpty: No CLOs defined for this course. Add outcomes in Academic → Subject → Curriculum map.
instructorPortal.reportsOutcomesNoClass: No active class sections found to export.
instructorPortal.prerequisiteHint: Students will not see this lesson until the prerequisite is completed.
instructorPortal.contentReleaseSubtitle: Control when and how students see content.
instructorPortal.defaultReleaseSettingsTitle: Default release settings for the course
instructorPortal.contentReleaseScheduleHint: Set when and how each lesson becomes available to students, and whether it is draft or published. Change the row, then click Save. Edit opens the lesson content page (materials, media, activities) — that is separate from these release settings.
instructorPortal.releaseDateUnusedImmediate: No date needed for immediate release.
instructorPortal.conditionOnlyForConditional: Use conditional release mode to add a rule here.
instructorPortal.defaultReleaseSettings: Default Release Settings for the Course
instructorPortal.noCoursesForContentRelease: No active courses assigned to you.
instructorPortal.integrityBanner: Open issue - CS201 Suspicion of cheating in Assignment 6 under review by Department Head
instructorPortal.previewModeAlert: Preview mode — This is what students will see after publishing. The lesson has not been published yet.
instructorPortal.lessonMediaUploadFailed: Upload failed. Check storage permissions and bucket settings.
instructorPortal.clo1Desc: The student can write a coherent academic paragraph
instructorPortal.clo2Desc: Analyzes academic texts and extracts main ideas
instructorPortal.clo3Desc: Uses effective listening skills in academic contexts
instructorPortal.clo4Desc: Produces professional oral presentations in English
instructorPortal.correctAnswerHint: Correct answer is highlighted in green
instructorPortal.uploadDragHint: Drag file here or click to upload
instructorPortal.assessmentWeightExceeded: Total assessment weight is {{total}}% — it cannot exceed 100%. Reduce weights before saving.
instructorPortal.shuffleQuestionsHint: Each student gets a different order
instructorPortal.randomFromBankHint: Set number of questions per tag
instructorPortal.resultAfterWindow: Show result after exam window ends
instructorPortal.resultManualRelease: Show result after manual release by instructor
instructorPortal.rubricAttachHelp: Rubrics are configured in the admin portal (Colleges → Rubric builder). Choose one to attach to this assessment.
instructorPortal.essayRequiresManual: Essay question — requires manual grading. You can attach a rubric.
instructorPortal.assignmentUploadHint: Upload a PDF or document for this assignment. The file is stored with the assessment (direct upload).
instructorPortal.matchingPairsHint: Enter pairs one per line: term A | term B
instructorPortal.correctAnswerIndices: Correct option index(es), comma-separated (0 = first line)
instructorPortal.importJsonHint: Import a JSON file: an array of objects with question_text, question_type, options (array), correct_answers (array of indices), estimated_marks.
instructorPortal.importFailed: Could not import. Use valid JSON (array of questions).
instructorPortal.excelImportHint: Use the downloaded template. Types: multiple_choice, true_false, matching, order, fill_blank, short_answer, essay, numeric, file_upload. Columns: question_text, question_type, option_a–d, correct_indices (MC/TF only; 0-based, comma-separated for multiple correct), difficulty_level, bloom_level, unit_number, estimated_marks, tags. For order, put items in option_a… in correct sequence.
instructorPortal.validationSelectCorrectTf: Select the correct answer for True/False (use the radio).
instructorPortal.validationSelectCorrectMc: Select at least one correct answer for multiple choice (use the checkboxes).
instructorPortal.validationOrderMinItems: Add at least two items for an ordering question.
instructorPortal.orderOptionsHint: Items are shown to students in random order; this list is the correct order (top = first).
instructorPortal.orderPlaceholderHint: e.g. Put these steps in the correct order.
instructorPortal.fillBlankPlaceholderHint: e.g. Water is composed of hydrogen and _____.
instructorPortal.numericPlaceholderHint: e.g. What is the result of 12 ÷ 4?
instructorPortal.fileUploadPlaceholderHint: e.g. Upload your lab report as a PDF.
instructorPortal.qbHintFillBlank: Use a blank line, _____ , or [blank] where the student should answer.
instructorPortal.qbHintNumeric: Numeric answer — set marks and grading rules in your assessment as needed.
instructorPortal.qbHintFileUpload: Students will upload a file — describe allowed formats in the prompt.
instructorPortal.qbHintMatching: One pair per line: left term | right term (see placeholder above).
instructorPortal.marksIncompleteWarning: Total marks not yet complete ({{current}}/{{total}}). Add more questions.
instructorPortal.submitFinalGradesToRecord: Submit Final Grades to Academic Record
instructorPortal.gradeSubmissionWarning: Important: After submitting grades to the academic record, they cannot be modified except through a formal request to the Head of Department. Please review all grades before proceeding.
instructorPortal.notesPlaceholder: Any special notes for the academic department...
instructorPortal.declarationText: I confirm that all entered grades are correct and final, and that I have reviewed all cases of students at risk of failure.
instructorPortal.submitGradesIrreversible: This action cannot be undone without the approval of the Department Head.
instructorPortal.reviewBeforeSubmit: Ensure their cases are reviewed before submission.
instructorPortal.gradeSheetUploadHint: Download the template, fill in component scores per student, then upload the file. Imported values appear in the table — click Save grades to store them.
instructorPortal.gradeSheetUploadSuccess: {{count}} student row(s) imported. Save to apply.
instructorPortal.saveBeforeSubmit: Save your grade changes before final submission.
instructorPortal.noStudentsInClass: No enrolled students in this class.
instructorPortal.searchStudent: Search by name or student ID…
instructorPortal.submitBlockedApprovals: All assessment groups must be approved before submission.

registerApplication.headerSubtitle: Complete the form below to apply for admission
registerApplication.collegeHint: Choose the college you wish to apply to
registerApplication.loadCollegesError: Failed to load colleges. Please refresh the page.

success.title: Application submitted successfully!
success.subtitle: Your application has been received. Please save your application number for tracking.
success.step1: Save your application number and date of birth to track your application status.
success.step2: You will receive an email confirmation shortly.
success.step3: Check your application status at any time from your dashboard.

scholarship.title: Scholarship
scholarship.intro: If you are applying for a scholarship, tell us about your request. You will upload supporting documents on the next step.
scholarship.requestLabel: I am applying for a scholarship
scholarship.typePlaceholder: e.g. Academic merit, financial need, athletic
scholarship.detailsPlaceholder: Describe your achievements, financial situation, or other reasons you should be considered…
scholarship.docIntro: Upload evidence for your scholarship request (letters, financial statement, recommendations). PDF or images, up to 10 MB each recommended.
scholarship.skipNoScholarship: You indicated you are not applying for a scholarship. Continue to general documents.
scholarship.requireOneDoc: Please upload at least one scholarship supporting document.

documents.title: General documents
documents.intro: You can upload these now or later from your application status page after submitting.

errors.step1Required: Please fill in all required fields in personal information
errors.submitFailed: Failed to submit application. Please try again.

fields.personalStatementPh: Tell us about yourself, your goals, and why you want to study at this university…
fields.notesPh: Any additional information you would like to provide…
fields.testScoresIntro: Please provide your standardized test scores (if applicable).
